checks.a_analysis: The output content closely follows the source text provided in the PDF. All paragraphs, headings, and table contents are derived directly from the source material. No evidence of fabricated body text or invented sections was found.

The source document does not contain any specific phone numbers, email addresses, or mailing addresses. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not introduce any fabricated contact details.

All three pages of the source document are accounted for in the output. The output includes the acronym list and items 1.1, 1.2, 1.3 from page 1; items 1.5, 1.6, 1.7, 1.8 from page 2; and items 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 from page 3. Item 1.4 is missing from the source itself, so its absence in the output is correct.

The tables in the output correctly represent the structure of the source document. Each goal item (e.g., 1.1, 1.2) and its associated task/committee rows are present. The conversion has correctly mapped the multi-row structure of the source tables into valid JSON table elements.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text within each table cell and section matches the source content. No significant paragraphs or sentences have been omitted. Key phrases such as committee roles (e.g., 'LGC – identify needs and practical ways to assist') are fully preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the output is faithful to the source. Minor OCR-related differences (like the use of dashes vs hyphens) or white-space adjustments in multi-line cells are within acceptable limits. The semantic meaning is preserved throughout.

checks.a_analysis: The text 'Goal 1. Improve, expand, and support statewide geospatial data and applications.' appears twice in the output (reading order 5 and 7), but this is reflecting the source document where the goal heading is repeated at the top of page 2. There are no other instances of duplicated content.

The heading hierarchy is mostly preserved with H1 for the title and H2 for the 'Committee Acronyms' and repeated 'Goal 1' heading. While Goal 1 (page 1) and Goal 2 (page 3) are rendered as paragraphs instead of headings, no levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3), which is the primary criterion for failure. The hierarchy is flat but valid.

The table structures in elements 6, 8, and 10 are corrupted. In the source document, the blue/gray section headers (e.g., '1.1', '1.2', '1.5', '2.1', etc.) span across the entire width of the table (2 columns). In the JSON output, these header rows are defined as single cells with 'colspan': 1, while the data rows immediately following them contain 2 cells. This creates an inconsistent column count within the tables, which is a structural failure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table sections 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.5, 1.6, 1.7, 1.8, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 all have full-width header bars spanning two columns.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 6, Row 1 (Header for 1.1) has "colspan": 1. Element 6, Row 2 (Data for 1.1) has two cells. This pattern repeats throughout all table elements (6, 8, 10).

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the output follows the logical progression of the source document page by page and section by section.
The list of committee acronyms on page 1 is rendered as a single paragraph with newlines. While it loses specific list formatting, the semantic grouping and meaning of the list are preserved.
There are no hyperlinks in the source document, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML document closely follows the source PDF. The document structure, including the acronym list and the series of tables representing different goals and sub-goals, is maintained in a single-column flow appropriate for HTML. The grouping of sub-goals into separate table blocks mirrors the page breaks in the PDF (e.g., Goal 1 is split into two tables just as it spans two pages in the source).

The tables in the HTML output are rendered correctly. They maintain the row and column structure seen in the PDF. Headers for sub-goals (e.g., 1.1, 1.2) are clearly distinguished from the content rows. The alignment of committee responsibilities (right column) with their corresponding goal descriptions (left column) is preserved and legible.
The source PDF is a text-only document with no meaningful images, logos, or charts. Therefore, no images are expected or missing in the HTML output.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While the 'Goal 1' heading appears twice, this is a faithful representation of the source PDF where the heading is repeated at the top of the second page. The table of contents at the beginning is an expected navigation feature and does not constitute content duplication.
